a:r.a:t: We're left with 17 gaps. RBAC has foundations, needs ABAC for data-level access. Memory v2 has the types, needs TTL enforcement and conflict resolution. Cost attribution is the gateway to enterprise — you can't chargeback without it. Audit logs are table stakes for compliance. PII redaction is GDPR minimum. Model governance prevents cowboys from shipping untested models. Tool versioning unblocks safe deployments. Prompt versioning enables AB testing. Human escalation is critical for high-stakes domains. Self-reflection prevents first-draft answers. Output guardrails stop PII/toxicity leaks. Red-teaming builds confidence. AST analysis locks down the sandbox. Skill signing secures the supply chain. Cross-session memory synthesis enables long-term learning. The sprint order is Critical first — human escalation, retention, RBAC→ABAC, AST sandbox, signing, cost attribution, audit log, PII. Then High-priority — routing, reflection, prompt versioning, tool versioning, escalation, output guards. Then Medium — conflict resolution, cross-session synthesis, cost model, red-teaming.
